cscBeginnerGuide.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Beginner Guide
cscBeginnerGuide.intro: Crime Scene Cleaner rewards players who understand the order of operations before diving into each job. Learn the cleanup loop, tool choices, and early habits that save time across every mission.

steps.title: Scan the Whole Scene First
steps.description: Before touching anything, walk through the full location. Each mission in Crime Scene Cleaner has a different mess layout, and scanning the scene first helps you spot hidden blood trails, evidence, and blockers before you start cleaning.

steps.title: Clear Bodies and Trash Early
steps.description: Body disposal and trash pickup open your movement lines and reveal hidden stains and evidence underneath. Doing this first reduces backtracking and helps you find items you would otherwise miss.

steps.title: Use the Right Tool for Each Surface
steps.description: The mop handles general bloodstains and open floor areas. The sponge is better for delicate objects, carpets, and furniture. The pressure washer works well on large surfaces but can cause extra mess if used carelessly near fragile setups.

steps.title: Manage Your Bucket
steps.description: Both the mop and sponge get dirty over time. Rinse them before they start spreading contamination instead of removing it. Fresh water resets your tool, but it also removes detergent buffs, so time your rinses carefully.

steps.title: Do a Full Sweep Before Leaving
steps.description: Completion in Crime Scene Cleaner is tracked per area. Before finishing a job, do a final walkthrough of every room including ceilings, high shelves, and furniture undersides. Hidden stains and missed items are the most common reason for lower scores.

steps.title: Invest in Core Upgrades First
steps.description: Upgrade your mop resistance, bucket capacity, and cleaning speed before spending on niche tools. These core improvements pay off across every mission and reduce friction on larger, more complex maps.

quickTips: Completion percentage is tracked per area, not per mission overall.
A dirty mop eventually spreads stains instead of removing them.
Check ceilings, high fixtures, and furniture surfaces — blood splatter appears everywhere.
Bucket rinsing removes detergent effects, so plan your bucket use before rinsing.

cscWalkthrough.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Walkthrough
cscWalkthrough.intro: Each Crime Scene Cleaner mission has its own layout, cleanup priorities, and secrets to find. Use this hub to understand what to expect from key missions and how to approach them efficiently.

cards.name: Bad Call
cards.description: The first main mission and the clearest introduction to the full cleanup loop. Focus on learning full-clear habits, check the attic and backroom carefully, and use this mission to build the foundations for larger maps.

cards.name: Trial by Blood
cards.description: Set in Miko's Spa and Wellness, this mission tests how well you switch between broad cleaning and careful detail work in a more layered, decorative interior. Track secrets and cassette pickups while the rooms are still fresh.

cards.name: Affair with Death
cards.description: The Mountainside Villa is one of the larger early jobs with five bodies and plenty of evidence. Route floor by floor instead of improvising and check locked side rooms as part of your main circuit.

cards.name: Amber Alert
cards.description: An Act 2 completion-heavy mission with collectibles, secrets, and multiple cleanup goals. Approach it like a 100 percent clear mission and track collectibles alongside main objectives.

milestones: Learn the cleanup loop in Bad Call
Clear Mountainside Villa with a floor-by-floor route
Reach full completion in Amber Alert

cscUpgradesGuide.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Upgrades Guide
cscUpgradesGuide.intro: The best upgrades in Crime Scene Cleaner are the ones that reduce friction on every single mission. Start with core tool improvements before spending on niche paths that only matter in specific situations.

items.name: Basic Mop Upgrades
items.description: The mop is your main all-purpose tool and affects blood cleanup on nearly every map. Improving mop performance reduces dirty-tool downtime and is the strongest early investment.

items.name: Bucket Upgrades
items.description: Bucket management is central because both mops and sponges rely on rinsing. Better bucket capacity and performance reduces resets and supports detergent use.

items.name: Sponge Upgrades
items.description: The sponge handles fragile objects, carpets, and furniture surfaces. It becomes more valuable as mission variety increases and you encounter more complex indoor layouts.

items.name: Additional Bucket
items.description: The base of the two-bucket strategy. Run one bucket with detergent for cleaning buffs and one with plain water for rinsing, which helps you hold detergent effects much longer.

items.name: Cleaning Speed
items.description: Speed upgrades affect the pace of nearly every mission and reduce time loss on larger jobs. Among the most recommended early skill picks across community guides.

items.name: Pressure Washer Path
items.description: Excellent for large open surfaces but more situational than mop and sponge upgrades. Best used in areas with low risk of creating extra mess from overspray.

items.name: Ozonator Utility
items.description: Weakens nearby stains and softens rooms while you clean elsewhere. A useful support tool on larger jobs, but not a first-buy priority compared to core cleaning improvements.

items.name: Detergent Investment
items.description: Detergents are strong but can be delayed if you use the two-bucket setup. Focus on raw cleaning tool efficiency first and add detergent depth once your core loop is stable.

cscMopSkills.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Mop Skills Guide
cscMopSkills.intro: The mop is the backbone of almost every cleanup run in Crime Scene Cleaner. A strong mop setup reduces rinsing interruptions, widens each cleaning pass, and keeps your loop efficient across long missions.

solutions.name: Increased Resistance
solutions.description: One of the standout mop skills because it extends how long the mop stays effective before getting too dirty to clean. Directly reduces downtime caused by forced rinsing.

solutions.name: Increased Range
solutions.description: Each cleaning pass covers more floor area, which adds up quickly in open-plan interiors with repeated stains across large surfaces.

solutions.name: Cleaning Speed
solutions.description: Frequently recommended because it improves the pace of routine stain removal across nearly every mission. Pairs well with players going for faster full clears.

solutions.name: Clean and Power Detergents
solutions.description: Detergent support improves stain handling and helps the mop stay effective for longer. Use with a dedicated detergent bucket to hold cleaning buffs through long sessions.

solutions.name: Careful Steps
solutions.description: A practical pick for players minimizing accidental rework, especially in cluttered indoor areas where pathing mistakes cost extra cleaning time.

solutions.name: Two-Bucket Setup
solutions.description: One bucket with detergent for buffs and one with plain water for rinsing. The most widely shared mop-adjacent optimization for maintaining a stronger cleaning loop over time.

managementTips: A dirty mop eventually creates new stains instead of removing them — rinse before this threshold.
Use one detergent bucket and one water bucket to hold cleaning buffs without losing them on every rinse.
Increased Range is especially valuable in open floor areas where coverage per pass matters most.
Build the mop setup first before committing to pressure washer or ozonator paths.

cscMoneyGuide.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Money Guide
cscMoneyGuide.intro: Money in Crime Scene Cleaner comes from clean job scores, collecting valuables, and finding hidden items across each location. Better payout comes from approaching missions with both cleanup quality and treasure hunting in mind.

cards.name: Collect All Valuables
cards.description: Every job location contains items worth money. Check drawers, cabinets, shelves, and under furniture. A complete search before leaving always reveals additional payouts.

cards.name: Find Hidden Items and Rooms
cards.description: Some maps have secret areas and puzzle-triggered spaces with additional valuables. Players who discover hidden rooms consistently earn more per mission.

cards.name: Aim for Higher Completion Ratings
cards.description: Score-based payout means a cleaner finish yields more money. Fewer missed spots and a higher overall rating translate directly into better mission payouts.

cards.name: Use the Achievement Route
cards.description: Following the Treasure and Strawberry Fields achievement paths is one of the most reliable ways to maximize cash earned in a single playthrough.

highlights: Check every drawer and cabinet before leaving
Explore all rooms including locked side areas
Higher completion ratings mean bigger payouts
Achievement routes often overlap with best money spots

cscPerfectScore.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Perfect Score Guide
cscPerfectScore.intro: A perfect score in Crime Scene Cleaner requires clearing everything in each area without leaving missed spots, uncollected items, or displaced furniture. Knowing what the game tracks is the first step to avoiding score penalties.

regions.name: Complete Blood and Stain Cleanup
regions.description: Every blood stain, splash, and surface contamination must be fully cleaned. Ceilings, walls, and above-eye-level surfaces are common spots players miss in their first pass.

regions.name: Remove All Bodies and Trash
regions.description: Body bags and all trash items must be fully removed from the scene. Partial removal does not count — the area must be completely cleared to avoid score deductions.

regions.name: Collect All Evidence and Valuables
regions.description: Evidence pieces, personal effects, and mission-specific collectibles must all be accounted for. Missing any piece affects your score and may lock certain completion bonuses.

regions.name: Restore Displaced Furniture and Objects
regions.description: Some missions require furniture to be returned to its original position or broken items replaced. Completing this fully adds to your final score and is required for certain achievements.

cscAchievements.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Achievements Guide
cscAchievements.intro: Crime Scene Cleaner has 80 Steam achievements covering mission completion, collectibles, tool use, exploration, and score targets. Many achievements can be combined into a single efficient run with the right route.

creatures.name: The Cleaner
creatures.description: Awarded for completing a mission with full cleanup. Unlocking this early sets the foundation for the True Cleaner and 100 percent completion paths.

creatures.name: Treasure
creatures.description: Requires finding a specific hidden item or collection across one of the main missions. Overlaps with the money farming route.

creatures.name: Strawberry Fields Forever
creatures.description: Connected to a hidden interaction or location in one of the story missions. Players following the treasure achievement route often unlock this in the same run.

creatures.name: 98 Percent
creatures.description: Linked to the common player experience of reaching near-complete cleanup and getting stuck. Usually caused by a missed stain above eye level or behind furniture.

creatures.name: Nightmare Survivor
creatures.description: Awarded for completing a mission on Nightmare difficulty. Requires mastering cleanup routing and resource management under stricter conditions.

creatures.name: Act 2 Complete
creatures.description: Unlocked after completing all Act 2 missions including the completion-heavy Amber Alert and its associated secrets.

cscSecrets.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Secrets Guide
cscSecrets.intro: Crime Scene Cleaner has hidden rooms, puzzle-triggered doors, and secret interactions across multiple missions. Many secrets overlap with collectibles, achievement routes, and hidden money spots.

items.name: Hidden Rooms
items.description: Several missions include rooms that are not visible on first glance. These are accessed through specific triggers, item interactions, or by finding concealed doorways behind furniture or walls.

items.name: Puzzle-Triggered Doors
items.description: Some areas require solving a specific environmental puzzle or finding an item before an alternate path opens. These are most common in the larger missions.

items.name: Cassette Tape Locations
items.description: Cassette tapes and CDs are hidden throughout each mission and are easy to miss on a standard clear. Many are in rooms that require you to clean first before the item becomes visible.

items.name: Evidence and Personal Effects
items.description: Some missions include personal effects or story-connected items that are not part of the main objective but contribute to the story and certain achievements.

items.name: Act 2 Hidden Content
items.description: Act 2 missions like Amber Alert have more secrets, collectibles, and environmental interactions than Act 1. A dedicated secrets pass before leaving is recommended for full completion.

unlockMilestones: Find first hidden room in any mission
Collect all cassette tapes in one mission
Unlock Act 2 secrets and CDs in Amber Alert

cscCassetteTapes.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Cassette Tapes Guide
cscCassetteTapes.intro: Cassette tapes and music CDs are hidden collectibles scattered across Crime Scene Cleaner missions. They are easy to miss during a standard cleanup run and often appear in areas that require careful searching.

sections.name: Act 1 Tape Locations
sections.description: Cassette tapes in Act 1 missions are usually placed in side rooms, on shelves, or in areas that become accessible after cleaning reveals the surrounding surface. Check each room before moving on.

sections.name: Act 2 CD Locations
sections.description: Act 2 includes additional music disc collectibles, particularly in the Amber Alert mission. The Act 2 secrets and CDs guide shows detailed locations for all collectibles in the new missions.

sections.name: Nightmare Mode Collectibles
sections.description: Cassette tapes appear in the Nightmare Mode variants of missions as well. The locations remain similar but the increased cleanup challenge makes finding them harder.

sections.name: Community Location Maps
sections.description: The Steam community and Reddit frequently share updated cassette tape location lists. Community guides are maintained per mission and updated when new content adds additional tapes.

growthMilestones: Find first cassette tape in any mission
Collect all tapes in a single Act 1 mission
Find all Act 2 CDs in Amber Alert
Complete the full collectibles set across all missions

cscBodiesEvidence.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Bodies and Evidence Guide
cscBodiesEvidence.intro: Body disposal and evidence collection are core objectives in Crime Scene Cleaner. Handling these efficiently opens movement lines, reveals hidden stains, and keeps mission completion on track.

priorities.name: Dispose of Bodies First
priorities.description: Body bags should be your first priority in any mission. Clearing bodies opens floor space, reveals hidden blood trails underneath, and makes the rest of the cleanup route faster.

priorities.name: Collect All Evidence
priorities.description: Evidence items are tracked separately from general cleanup. Missing any piece affects your score and blocks certain completion achievements. Search each room thoroughly.

priorities.name: Clear Trash and Debris
priorities.description: Trash and general debris must also be removed. Clear it alongside body disposal so you can access all floor areas and stains underneath.

priorities.name: Check for Hidden Evidence
priorities.description: Some evidence items are tucked behind furniture, in drawers, or in rooms that are only accessible after cleaning the primary area. Do not assume a room is complete after the first pass.

priorities.name: Restore Personal Effects
priorities.description: Some missions include personal items that need to be returned to their original placement. These are part of the mission completion requirements and affect the final score.

priorities.name: Track Room by Room
priorities.description: Keep track of which rooms have been cleared and which still need bodies or evidence removed. A room-by-room approach prevents backtracking on larger multi-room missions.

cscTrueCleaner.title: Crime Scene Cleaner True Cleaner Guide
cscTrueCleaner.intro: Achieving a full clean rating in Crime Scene Cleaner means leaving nothing behind — no stains, no bodies, no evidence, no debris. This guide breaks down what the game checks in each category.

achievements.title: Blood Stains
achievements.description: Clean all blood and organic stains from every floor section. Use Cleaner Sense to reveal stains in dark or hard-to-see areas.

achievements.title: Dirt and Debris
achievements.description: General dirt, mud, and organic debris must be fully removed. Check corners and under furniture for missed patches.

achievements.title: Splatter Above Eye Level
achievements.description: Blood splatter reaches walls and ceilings in high-velocity scenes. Look up regularly and use the sponge for hard-to-reach vertical surfaces.

achievements.title: High Shelves and Fixtures
achievements.description: Furniture tops and high shelves can collect contamination. Scan above eye level in every room before marking it complete.

achievements.title: Contaminated Furniture
achievements.description: Upholstered furniture and decorative objects can hold stains. Use the sponge rather than the mop to avoid breaking fragile items.

achievements.title: Displaced and Broken Items
achievements.description: Restore furniture to its original position and replace broken items where required. Missing this category is a common reason for incomplete scores.

achievements.title: All Bodies Bagged
achievements.description: Every body must be fully bagged and removed from the scene. A single missed body prevents a full completion rating.

achievements.title: Evidence and Personal Effects
achievements.description: All evidence items and personal effects must be collected. Check rooms after cleaning since items can be hidden under removed stains.

cscNightmareMode.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Nightmare Mode Guide
cscNightmareMode.intro: Nightmare Mode in Crime Scene Cleaner adds additional challenges to existing missions, requiring more precise cleanup and stricter completion standards.

faqs.question: What is Nightmare Mode in Crime Scene Cleaner?
faqs.answer: Nightmare Mode is a harder variant of Crime Scene Cleaner's main missions that introduces additional cleanup requirements, stricter scoring, and more complex mission conditions compared to the standard version.

faqs.question: How do I unlock Nightmare Mode in Crime Scene Cleaner?
faqs.answer: Nightmare Mode becomes available after completing the standard version of a mission. Progress through the main mission list first before attempting the Nightmare variant of any location.

faqs.question: Is the cleanup area the same in Crime Scene Cleaner Nightmare Mode?
faqs.answer: The base layout is similar, but Nightmare Mode typically has more stains, additional cleanup targets, or environmental changes that increase the difficulty of achieving a full clear.

faqs.question: Are there exclusive Crime Scene Cleaner achievements for Nightmare Mode?
faqs.answer: Yes. Several Crime Scene Cleaner achievements are tied to completing missions in Nightmare Mode. Check the achievements list for specific unlocks associated with Nightmare completion.

faqs.question: What is the best strategy for Crime Scene Cleaner Nightmare Mode?
faqs.answer: Master the standard version of a mission first and develop a reliable room-by-room route. Nightmare Mode rewards players who already have efficient cleanup habits from their regular playthroughs.

cscAct2Walkthrough.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Act 2 Walkthrough
cscAct2Walkthrough.intro: Act 2 expands Crime Scene Cleaner with new missions, more complex layouts, and additional secrets. Here is what players need to know about tackling the Act 2 content.

faqs.question: What new missions are in Crime Scene Cleaner Act 2?
faqs.answer: Act 2 adds new main missions including Amber Alert, which is one of the most completion-heavy jobs in the game, with secrets, multiple CDs, and layered cleanup objectives.

faqs.question: How does Crime Scene Cleaner Act 2 differ from Act 1?
faqs.answer: Act 2 missions tend to be larger and more layered, with more collectibles, tougher cleanup requirements, and additional secrets compared to the Act 1 missions.

faqs.question: What is the Amber Alert mission in Crime Scene Cleaner?
faqs.answer: Amber Alert is the flagship Act 2 mission. It requires a full completion approach with room-by-room tracking, collectibles, and multiple objectives beyond standard cleanup.

faqs.question: Are there new secrets and CDs in Crime Scene Cleaner Act 2?
faqs.answer: Yes. Act 2 introduces new CD collectibles and secret interactions across its missions. The Amber Alert mission has a dedicated secrets and CDs guide for full completion.

faqs.question: When was Crime Scene Cleaner Act 2 released?
faqs.answer: Act 2 was released as a major content update to Crime Scene Cleaner on Steam. It expanded the game with new missions, a Nightmare Mode variant, and additional collectibles.

cscAct2Secrets.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Act 2 Secrets and CDs Guide
cscAct2Secrets.intro: Act 2 contains additional hidden collectibles, secret rooms, and music CDs across its missions. This guide focuses on what to find, where to look, and how to approach a full collectibles run.

settings.name: Amber Alert Hidden Rooms
settings.description: Amber Alert contains multiple hidden rooms accessed through environmental triggers. Check specific furniture, panels, and interactive objects throughout the location.

settings.name: Amber Alert CD Locations
settings.description: Music CDs in Amber Alert are placed in side rooms, behind unlocked doors, and in areas that become accessible after partial cleanup of the main area.

settings.name: Act 2 Secret Interactions
settings.description: Certain story-connected objects and personal effects in Act 2 missions trigger additional story context or unlock secret interactions beyond the main cleanup objective.

settings.name: Nightmare Mode Secrets
settings.description: Nightmare Mode variants of Act 2 missions contain the same secrets but are harder to access due to the increased cleanup requirements. Plan the secrets route as part of the overall mission strategy.

cscDetergents.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Detergents Guide
cscDetergents.intro: Detergents in Crime Scene Cleaner improve how effectively your tools clean stains and surfaces. Choosing the right mix and managing your buckets properly makes a noticeable difference on larger missions.

entries.title: Clean Detergent
entries.description: The standard detergent that improves general stain removal across most surfaces. A good starting option for early missions before more advanced options become available.

entries.title: Power Detergent
entries.description: A stronger formula for tougher stains and larger surface areas. Best used with the mop on open floors where broad cleaning efficiency matters most.

entries.title: Two-Bucket Strategy
entries.description: Run one bucket with detergent for cleaning buffs and a separate bucket with plain water for rinsing. This preserves your detergent longer and gives you more control over when to reset your cleaning tool.

entries.title: Detergent Timing
entries.description: Apply detergent before tackling the densest stain areas in each room. Reapplying mid-clean interrupts your rhythm and wastes mixing time on large missions.

cscSystemInfo.title: Crime Scene Cleaner System Requirements and Updates
cscSystemInfo.intro: Crime Scene Cleaner is available on Steam for PC, Xbox, and PS5. This module covers PC system requirements and the game's major update history.

steps.title: PC Minimum Requirements
steps.description: Crime Scene Cleaner requires a mid-range PC for stable performance. The game launched on Steam on August 14, 2024 and is available for Windows with controller and keyboard support.

steps.title: PC Recommended Setup
steps.description: For the smoothest experience, a dedicated GPU with at least 8GB VRAM is recommended. The game runs at a stable frame rate on most modern PC hardware at recommended settings.

steps.title: Act 2 Update
steps.description: The Act 2 update added new missions including Amber Alert, expanded the story content, and introduced Nightmare Mode difficulty variants for existing maps.

steps.title: Platform Updates
steps.description: Crime Scene Cleaner has received updates across Steam, Xbox, and PS5 including quality of life improvements, bug fixes, and localization updates for additional languages.

steps.title: Reporting Issues
steps.description: For bugs or technical issues, the official Discord server and the Steam Community discussions page are the recommended channels for reporting problems and getting support.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Crime Scene Cleaner

questions.question: What platforms support Crime Scene Cleaner?
questions.answer: Crime Scene Cleaner is available on Steam for PC, Xbox, and PlayStation 5. All versions include the full campaign with Act 1 and Act 2 content.

questions.question: How many achievements does Crime Scene Cleaner have?
questions.answer: Crime Scene Cleaner has 80 Steam achievements covering mission completion, secrets, collectibles, tool upgrades, and difficulty challenges.

questions.question: Does Crime Scene Cleaner have Nightmare Mode?
questions.answer: Yes. Crime Scene Cleaner includes a Nightmare Mode that adds harder cleanup requirements and stricter scoring to existing missions.

questions.question: What is the best mop upgrade in Crime Scene Cleaner?
questions.answer: Increased Resistance and Cleaning Speed are the most recommended mop upgrades in Crime Scene Cleaner. They reduce downtime from dirty tools and improve overall cleanup pace.

questions.question: How do I get a perfect score in Crime Scene Cleaner?
questions.answer: A perfect score in Crime Scene Cleaner requires cleaning all blood and stains, disposing of all bodies, collecting all evidence, and restoring displaced furniture.

questions.question: How many copies has Crime Scene Cleaner sold?
questions.answer: Crime Scene Cleaner has sold over 1 million copies. The game launched on Steam in August 2024 and has maintained a very positive rating from over 13,000 English reviews.

cta.description: Join the Crime Scene Cleaner community on Discord for guides, updates, and discussions!
